Elvia.Edna.Events.Cli 1.0.0+f0f11c5c379fa63009d01d7a84ba6adf5f7209d7
Copyright (C) 2026 Elvia.Edna.Events.Cli

  avro-create                    Create an Avro schema for a .NET type.

  code-generate                  Generate C# code for a schema from the Schema
                                 Registry or stdin.

  registry-get                   Retrieve a schema from the Schema Registry.

  registry-test                  Verify that a .NET type is compatible with a
                                 schema in the Schema Registry.

  registry-versions-get          Retrieve versions of schema from the Schema
                                 Registry.

  registry-delete                Delete schema for a given topic.

  kafka-topic-delete             Delete a given topic. Must be recreated
                                 manually in Terraform.

  kafka-topic-messages-delete    Delete messages in a given topic. Only if NOT
                                 using compacting.

  kafka-metadata-get             List Kafka metadata.

  consumergroup-offset-change    Set offset for consumer group

  consumergroup-delete           Delete a consumer group

  help                           Display more information on a specific command.

  version                        Display version information.

Elvia.Edna.Events.Cli 1.0.0+f0f11c5c379fa63009d01d7a84ba6adf5f7209d7
Copyright (C) 2026 Elvia.Edna.Events.Cli
USAGE:
Create a schema for a built-in type:
  elvia-edna-events avro-create --type System.DateTime
Create a schema for a type in a compiled assembly:
  elvia-edna-events avro-create --assembly ./out/Example.Models.dll --type
  Example.Models.ExampleModel
Create a schema for a type in a compiled assembly with dependencies:
  elvia-edna-events avro-create --assembly System.Text.Json
  ./out/Example.Models.dll --type Example.Models.ExampleModel

  -a, --assembly           The name of or path to an assembly to load (multiple
                           space-separated values accepted).

  --enum-behavior          The type of schema that enum types should be
                           represented by. Options are "symbolic" (generate an
                           "enum" schema; the default behavior), "integral"
                           (generate an "int" or "long" schema based on the
                           underlying type; the behavior for all flag enums),
                           and "nominal" (generate a "string" schema).

  --nullable-references    (Default: Annotated) Which reference types should be
                           represented with nullable union schemas. Options are
                           "annotated" (use nullable annotations if available;
                           the default behavior), "none", and "all".

  --temporal-behavior      Whether timestamps should be represented with
                           "string" schemas (ISO 8601) or "long" schemas
                           (timestamp logical types). Options are "iso8601",
                           "epochmilliseconds", and "epochmicroseconds".

  -t, --type               Required. The type to build a schema for.

  --help                   Display this help screen.

  --version                Display version information.

USAGE:
Generate code for an Elvia topic using system not owning the topic.:
  elvia-edna-events code-generate --system mdmx --topic
  public.msi.msim.meteringpointversions
Generate code for an Elvia topic using system in topic.:
  elvia-edna-events code-generate --system msim --topic
  public.msi.msim.meteringpointversions

  --nullable-references    (Default: true) Whether reference types selected for
                           nullable record fields should be annotated as
                           nullable.

  -c, --registry-config    Configuration options to provide to the registry
                           client (multiple space-separated key=value pairs
                           accepted).

  -r, --registry-url       The URL of the schema registry.

  -i, --id                 If a subject/version/topic is not specified, the ID
                           of the schema.

  -s, --subject            If an ID is not specified, the subject of the schema.

  -v, --version            The version of the schema.

  --topic                  The value subject topic name

  --system                 The system/namespace for the consuming application.
                           Used for fetching schema registry credentials from
                           Vault.

  --help                   Display this help screen.

  --version                Display version information.

               or groups)?

  --help       Display this help screen.

  --version    Display version information.

  --registry-url http://registry:8081 --system edna --type
  Example.Models.ExampleModel --topic private.dp.edna.example

  -a, --assembly           The name of or path to an assembly to load (multiple
                           space-separated values accepted).

  -t, --type               Required. The type to test.

  -c, --registry-config    Configuration options to provide to the registry
                           client (multiple space-separated key=value pairs
                           accepted).

  -r, --registry-url       The URL of the schema registry.

  -i, --id                 If a subject/version/topic is not specified, the ID
                           of the schema.

  -s, --subject            If an ID is not specified, the subject of the schema.

  -v, --version            The version of the schema.

  --topic                  The value subject topic name

  --system                 The system/namespace for the consuming application.
                           Used for fetching schema registry credentials from
                           Vault.

  --help                   Display this help screen.
